ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lee Kaster, via zoom, for Karen Fisher, Administrator.

Mr. Kaster reports of an Accounting Petition and Inventory and Appraisal pending in the court's efling queue.

The Status on Close of Estate is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Teebay. Not reported.

Petition For Authorization To Retain Litigation Counsel And For Approval Of Proposed Attorneyclient Fee Agreement
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lee Kaster appeared by zoom for Karen Fisher (Petitioner/Administrator).

The Court noted that this matter is on calendar for approval of continuation of a contract for legal services, that fees started to be incurred in mid-2017, and stated that there is over $10,000.00 in interest and that it seems like a lot of interest. Mr. Kaster stated that the Decedent retained the law firm in 2017 and the case has been in litigation since 2018, that the agreements are not different and that the important terms are exactly the same. Mr. Kaster also stated that the Client has the option to pay as they go without paying interest, that they can do financing through a lender, that the majority of the costs were incurred while the Decedent was alive, and that the rate is LIBOR plus a certain percentage (6%). In addition, Mr. Kaster stated that the trial is set in July, that they are close to the end of the case, that they had mediation on March 19th, that they reached a settlement in principal between the estate and the Housing Authority, and that he cannot provide any information regarding the settlement amount because the Housing Authority has requested that the amount be kept confidential. The Petition for Authorization to Retain Litigation Counsel and for Approval of Proposed Attorney Client Fee Agreement is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authorization To Retain Litigation Counsel And For Approval Of Proposed Attorneyclient Fee Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Mar-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Record
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lee Kaster appeared by zoom/telephone.

Mr. Kaster presented oral argument to support his position. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The Petitioner's Motion to Seal Proposed Fee Agreement is denied. For the purposes of deciding whether to seal court records, the party seeking the sealing of the documents bears the burden of presenting information sufficient to identify the specific information claimed to be entitled to such treatment and to identify the nature of the harm threatened by disclosure. (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) The party must set forth specific facts in support of a motion to seal; statements that are too conclusory, abstract, or generic are not sufficient to satisfy the burden. (Id. at 895-896.) If specific facts have not been enumerated demonstrating the harm that would result if the documents are made part of the public record, then there is no basis for the court to conclude that the interest in keeping the records confidential overrides the public's interest in having access to court records. (Id. at 896-898.)
A court may order that a record be filed under seal only if it expressly finds facts that establish: (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC 2.550(d); NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218). The instant motion does not set forth specific facts to support each of these five elements. Accordingly, it is denied..

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
